name: git-guard
description: PreToolUse(Bash) git-push safety guard, implemented in hexa-lang (`_git_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run` — no Python, no shell shim). (1) DENIES force-type push: `git push --force` / `-f` · `--force-with-lease` · `git push <remote> +<ref>` (refspec-level force); quotes stripped before tokenizing (0.4.2). (2) Non-blocking STALE-BASE PUSH advisory (0.5.0): for a non-force push, emits additionalContext when the current branch trails the remote default base (origin/HEAD, else origin/main) by >= 20 commits — the shared-workdir branch-swap / silent-revert fingerprint; push still proceeds. (3) Non-blocking STALE-BASE MERGE advisory (0.6.0, anima #1105): a `gh pr merge` (squash) reaches main via a path the push-time check never saw — it revert-deleted 35190 files in #1105 — so when the command contains `gh pr merge` (no `git push`, not cross-repo `--repo X`), runs the same divergence probe and warns before the merge lands (real deny on mass deletion lives in pr-cycle-hook's deletion-sanity gate). Hook-bypass (--no-verify) NOT blocked — left to user discipline. NO opt-out by design — no env var, no config file, no exception list.
